DP Huber noisy gradient descent for a scalar mean

Source: R/helper_scree.R
dp_huber_noisy_gd.Rd

Internal helper that implements a scalar Huber-type differentially private mean estimator via noisy gradient descent. The optional argument scale_factor is useful when the released quantity should be a scaled version of the mean, such as the \(n/(n-1)\)-adjusted scree convention.

Usage

dp_huber_noisy_gd(w, eps_gd, delta_gd, tau, T, mu0 = 0, eta0 = 1)

Arguments

w

Numeric vector representing the one-dimensional sample.

eps_gd

Positive privacy epsilon allocated to the noisy-gradient-descent stage.

delta_gd

Positive privacy delta allocated to the noisy-gradient-descent stage.

tau

Positive Huber threshold.

T

Positive integer number of gradient-descent iterations.

mu0

Initial value for the iterative procedure.

eta0

Base step size.

Value

Numeric scalar representing the final private estimate.
